title: Key concepts: golden age of Islam
description: Test your knowledge on the golden age of Islam!
topic_path: Key concepts: golden age of Islam

assessment_items.question_content:
The Abbasid Caliphate built which entirely new city as its capital?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Abbasid Caliphate didn't want its capital to be located in the same place as the previous caliphate, the Umayyad, which was Damascus.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why was Baghdad in such a strategically good location for trade, cultural exchange, and able to support a large population? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Baghdad is not near the Americas.

choices.content: Its central location between Asia and the Americas
Its proximity to the Tigris and Euphrates rivers and therefore food
Its proximity to the Baltic Sea and therefore fish and furs

hints: The House of Wisdom was not created during the Umayyad dynasty.

choices.content: created under the Umayyad dynasty as a place where Persian scholars could study
created by Abbasid caliphs as a place where only Arab or Persian scholars were allowed to work
created by Abbasid caliphs as a center of learning for people of many religions and backgrounds

assessment_items.question_content:
The primary goal of the **translation movement** was to


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Scholars were primarily translating texts into Arabic.

assessment_items.question_content:
Historians call this the Golden Age of Islam because, during this time, 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Abbasids did not find wealth underneath the city.

choices.content: scholars preserved the knowledge from ancient texts and fostered new innovations
there were no breakouts of war or conflict in the Islamic world
the Abbasid caliphs discovered a massive source of wealth under the city of Baghdad
